title: ceratopteris oblongiloba
intro: Ceratopteris oblongiloba, also known as the water fern or water clover, is a type of aquatic fern native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Asian cultures, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Ceratopteris oblongiloba is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
